programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: RAMEN JAPAN
programme.desc: Hakodate and Asahikawa ramen, enjoy a hot helping of both! College kids give their all to a summer of ramen.

programme.title: Time-lapse Japan
programme.desc: A 700-year-old hot spring, Shirabu Onsen, is maintained via daily trips up the slopes of the 2,000-meter mountain where it sits. See the hot spring's guardian at work, in gorgeous time-lapse footage.

programme.title: Where We Call Home
programme.desc: We meet two men in Shimane. In Unnan City, Korean Sanghyun uses local rice to brew makgeolli like it's done in his homeland. In Tamatsukuri Onsen, Nepalese Nandeshwar tends the front desk of an inn.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Somewhere Street
programme.desc: A 15-minute spin-off of the "Somewhere Street" series, "A Short Stroll" explores cities through a traveler's eyes. Today, we visit Budapest, the capital of Hungary, known as the "Pearl of the Danube."

programme.title: Nun's Cookbook
programme.desc: The temple chefs make butterbur miso. Just boil butterbur shoots, then grind with sugar, mirin and white miso. The taste is slightly bitter, but perfect for early spring.

programme.title: Hometown Stories
programme.sub-title: A Doctor Duo: Songs to Comfort the Heart
programme.desc: Two doctors in western Japan have an alter ego as the music duo Insheart, whose songs bring hope to patients and families. We follow them and the fans who find strength and comfort in their music.

programme.title: Satoyama Kitchen Diary
programme.sub-title: Veggies Patiently Pickled for the Cold Season
programme.desc: Hi! It's me, Tama. In winter, I never stray far away from my warm friend, Mr. Stove. But Grandpa Makoto and Grandma Nobuko are off to see our neighbor to learn how to make pickles. Not me. I'm staying right here. The only thing I need to know about pickles is how to eat them!

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: CINEMATIC JOURNEY
programme.sub-title: Hiking the San'in Coast Endless Beauty
programme.desc: The San'in Kaigan Geopark Trail is a 230-kilometer-long course connecting the Sea of Japan coastline along Tottori, Hyogo, and Kyoto prefectures, which was designated as a UNESCO Global Geopark. Canadian actor Kyle Card embarks on a journey through time to uncover the ancient forces that shaped this coastline.

programme.title: Time-lapse Journey
programme.desc: Iwate Prefecture is home to many historic sites which were made to represent Buddhist paradise. Chuson-ji Temple in Hiraizumi, a World Heritage Site, is home to the golden Konjikido hall which offers a prayer for peace. The garden in Motsu-ji Temple was made as a depiction of paradise and has been also registered as a World Heritage Site. Catch a glimpse of eternity through time-lapse photography.

programme.title: Magical Japanese
programme.desc: This episode focuses on me (eye). Just like other languages, Japanese has a lot of words and phrases related to eyes, and they are often used in daily life.

programme.title: Easy Japanese
programme.sub-title: #11 Do you have any lucky charms?
programme.desc: If you want to ask if a shop has a certain item, say the item's name and "wa arimasu ka."

programme.title: Culture Crossroads
programme.desc: A Dutchman makes traditional washi paper, growing the raw materials and even creating artworks with it. His mission is to add new value to washi and forge a path forward into the future.

programme.title: HELLO! NHK WORLD-JAPAN
programme.desc: In a special feature on Hokkaido, programs looked at the ecology of brown bears, a special bento lunch, and a bountiful landscape formed by rugged natural conditions and pioneering development.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Wild Hokkaido!
programme.desc: Two cycling guides ride on snowy roads toward a mountain lake. Passing by railroad remnants, they reach the frozen-over and snow-covered Lake Nukabira, where cycling on the ice is permitted in winter.

programme.title: The Masterpieces of Ukiyo-e producer Tsutaju
programme.desc: This miniseries presents the masterpieces of Tsutaju, a brilliant ukiyo-e producer of the Edo period. This episode takes a close look at artist Utamaro's iconic "Woman with a Glass Noisemaker."

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.sub-title: Exquisite Creations Inspired by Nature: Hosono Hitomi / Ceramic Artist
programme.desc: Hosono Hitomi's botanically themed ceramics are highly acclaimed and held in museum collections across the world. She talks about her intricate designs and unmatched technique.

programme.title: One in Every Japanese
programme.desc: A hike can turn unsafe very quickly. In 2023, Japan saw a record high of 2,761 mountain accidents. We follow a veteran hiker lost and injured for six days, revealing how he ultimately survived.

programme.title: One in Every Japanese
programme.desc: In Japan 6.7 million or one in 19 seniors aged 65+ live alone. In a remote village, the only grocery shop supports the elderly living alone. We find out why one widowed woman is happiest now.

programme.title: Ukiyoe EDO-LIFE
programme.sub-title: Braving the Rain and the Currents
programme.desc: Caught in an evening shower, Edo townspeople scramble across a bridge in search of shelter. On the river beyond, a lone figure steers his boat, unfazed by the rain. What is his destination? *"Sudden Shower over Shin-Ohashi Bridge and Atake," from the series "One Hundred Famous Views of Edo" (1857) by Utagawa Hiroshige

programme.title: Backstage Japan
programme.desc: We explore the Tokyo Skytree, from its dazzling heights to its hidden depths. Discover the role this iconic structure plays in advancing scientific research.

programme.title: Seasoning the Seasons
programme.desc: Hakodate, a port city located in southern Hokkaido, is known for having one of the best night views in Japan. As the surrounding seas darken, the lights of the city warm the hearts of dwellers. Beneath each light lies the lives of citizens. As the winter cold chills Hakodate, the city shines even brighter.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Somewhere Street
programme.desc: A 15-minute spin-off of the "Somewhere Street" series, "A Short Stroll" explores cities through travelers' eyes. Today we visit Mongolia's capital, Ulaanbaatar, a city in the midst of modern change.

programme.title: Medical Frontiers
programme.sub-title: Gut Bacteria for Parkinson's and ADHD
programme.desc: Fecal microbiota transplantation transfers gut bacteria from a healthy donor's stool into a patient's colon. It is being explored as a treatment for ulcerative colitis, Parkinson's disease and ADHD.

programme.title: MATSURI: The Heartbeat of Japan
programme.desc: On New Year's Eve, performers dress as terrifying monsters called namahage. They barge into people's homes, condemning laziness and frightening children. But this good-natured tradition strengthens family and community bonds. In Tayazawa, a small village in Akita Prefecture, the practice came to an end several years ago, but was recently restarted by an enthusiastic group of locals. They made costumes and succeeded in reviving a custom at risk of slipping away.

programme.title: Nun's Cookbook
programme.desc: The temple chefs show us how to make su-no-mono with dried daikon radish. Just mix rehydrated daikon with red radish and sweet vinegar, and this tasty pink-colored dish is ready to serve.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE

programme.title: Herbal Symphony
programme.desc: Winter in Japan's oldest private herb garden in Uda, Nara Prefecture. As fallen leaves and withered grass cover the ground, the search begins for treasures hidden beneath the frozen soil.

programme.title: Time-lapse Japan
programme.desc: A 700-year-old hot spring, Shirabu Onsen, is maintained via daily trips up the slopes of the 2,000-meter mountain where it sits. See the hot spring's guardian at work, in gorgeous time-lapse footage.

programme.title: Journeys in Japan
programme.sub-title: Hokkaido: Voices from the Northern Land
programme.desc: Through the generations, Hokkaido has been home to people from diverse backgrounds, who have developed their livelihoods and established distinctive cultures in each area. Confronting the harsh natural environment of this vast northern island, the inhabitants have poured their feelings — joy, anxiety, prayers and wishes — into songs that are cherished to the present day. Those songs continue to bring local communities together through shared memories and values. On this edition of Journeys in Japan, British photographer Alfie Goodrich visits areas of Hokkaido that are home to the following songs: Esashi Oiwake, Soran Bushi, Tokachi Uma-uta, Hokkai Bon-uta and the Upopo songs of the Ainu people. Through this soundscape so deeply rooted in each community, he explores Hokkaido's history, culture and local traditions.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.sub-title: Connecting the World Through Board Games: Hayashi Hisashi / Game Designer
programme.desc: In July 2025, Hayashi Hisashi became the first Japanese winner of a prestigious German award for the best board game of the year. How does he design games that captivate players around the world?

programme.title: One in Every Japanese
programme.desc: In 2023, one in 27 children in Japan was not attending school, a record high. But children at one free school enjoy themselves. Seeing them thrive reminds adults to see matters from a child's view.

programme.title: One in Every Japanese

programme.sub-title: Demining Ukraine: Uncovering an Invisible Enemy

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.sub-title: Considering Our Future Through Dinosaur Research: Kobayashi Yoshitsugu / Paleontologist / Professor, Hokkaido University
programme.desc: In June 2025, Kobayashi Yoshitsugu published a trailblazing paper, illuminating the evolutionary process of large tyrannosaurs. He explains how such research helps us consider the future of humanity.

programme.title: IMPACTS: Climates Change the World
programme.desc: The entire Earth is affected by climate change, occurring with a greater speed and scale than ever before. This program uses scientific data and the latest findings to raise awareness on its impact.

programme.title: Ukiyoe EDO-LIFE
programme.sub-title: Eat, Drink and Enjoy the Flowers
programme.desc: We visit one of the most famous cherry blossom spots in Edo. Flower viewing parties were for eating, drinking and making merry. It was also an opportunity for young men and women to mingle. *"Flower Viewing at Mimeguri Shrine" (1799) by Kitagawa Utamaro

programme.title: Medical Frontiers
programme.sub-title: Gut Bacteria for Parkinson's and ADHD
programme.desc: Fecal microbiota transplantation transfers gut bacteria from a healthy donor's stool into a patient's colon. It is being explored as a treatment for ulcerative colitis, Parkinson's disease and ADHD.

programme.title: Hit the Road
programme.desc: Come on a scenic drive through Iwate Prefecture's rich nature. Starting from Morioka station, we head to the picturesque Jodogahama Beach. Along the way we make a detour to Tono, known for its kappa legends.

programme.title: Through the Kitchen Window
programme.desc: In a small fishing town, Wajima Eiko crafts wonderful dishes from the unsold catch at the fish market where she has worked for over 50 years.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE

programme.title: Design × Stories

programme.desc: A selection of acclaimed programs from NHK's domestic channels dubbed into English.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Time and Tide
programme.desc: Time and Tide transcends time to detail extraordinary historical events in Japan and the world. Join NHK WORLD-JAPAN on a journey that reveals the truth behind some of the turning points of the past.

programme.title: Magical Japanese
programme.desc: The Japanese language is rich in words and expressions influenced by nature, history and culture. This episode looks at words related to the morning. Japanese people cherish the subtle changes in nature, and they have been particularly fond of the quiet hours in the early morning. The moment of sunrise alone has produced many beautiful expressions. Poet, literary translator, and long-time Japan resident Peter MacMillan guides us through unique words and the culture behind them.

programme.title: MATSURI: The Heartbeat of Japan
programme.desc: Kawanishi Dainenbutsu Kenbai is an elegant and dynamic dance, accompanied by flutes and taiko drums. In 2022, it was registered as UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, and it even takes place at a World Heritage Site: Chusonji in Hiraizumi, Iwate Prefecture. The dance retells a story from 900 years ago, when the Buddha, in the form of a monkey, guided restless souls to the Pure Land. We meet local people who are working hard to keep the tradition alive.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Spiritual Explorers
programme.desc: In the tenth month of the lunar calendar, Shinto deities gather in Izumo to discuss the intertwining of fates. We explore Japan's spirit of harmony through this ritual.

programme.title: National Parks of Japan
programme.desc: Daisen-Oki National Park comprises islands and a mainland area with a coast and mountain range. Home to shrines and sacred mountains, it's been the setting of Japanese myths. Join us on a cruise with spectacular sights and a trek across the mainland, where we observe a daily morning ritual at Miho Shrine and climb the majestic Mt. Daisen.

programme.title: BOSAI: Science that Can Save Your Life
programme.desc: Be earthquake-ready! Learn survival tips for quakes and when you're trapped under rubble. You'll want to watch this before your next trip to Japan.

programme.title: DESIGN MUSEUM JAPAN
programme.desc: Yamagata Prefecture / Nakui Naoko (Book designer) Thread for traditional "shina" fabric takes a year to make. Ultrafine mohair is also made here, with a 60-year-old device. Nakui admires the hunt for improvement.

programme.title: Darwin's Amazing Animals
programme.desc: The Darwin team's cameras eye some mysterious trees in the African desert featuring massive dangling bundles of dry grass. These amazing ecosystems reveal their secrets for 720 consecutive hours.

programme.title: Time-lapse Journey
programme.desc: Kamikochi is a beautiful mountainous region in Nagano Prefecture that's 1,500m above sea level. One famous spot here is Taisho Pond. Its clear waters reflect the mountains in a captivating way. In the winter, the region is even more beautiful as sunlight shines on the rime ice covering the trees. See the frozen beauty of Kamikochi through time-lapse photography.

programme.title: Core Kyoto
programme.sub-title: Alleyways: The Age-Old Wisdom of Town Planning
programme.desc: Kyoto has about 13,000 alleys with a total distance of 940km, and an increasing number of their houses are unoccupied. People are working on projects and tours to preserve and revitalize these areas.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Outside In: Lost in Academia
programme.desc: American linguistic anthropologist Debra Occhi dives into the fascinating world of mascots and characters. From adorable to heroic, she explores how their naming is shaped by sound.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: The Factory Wonders
programme.desc: Our team visits a heat-resistant glass factory in Koga, Ibaraki Pref., to witness the skills of the glassblowers and lampworkers, and learn about the secrets that heat-resistant glass holds.

programme.title: JAPAN FROM ABOVE: UP CLOSE / Co-production between NHK, Gedeon Programmes, ZDF Arte, and Voyage
programme.desc: In this episode, we visit the snow-covered region of Aomori Prefecture and travel along the famous Tsugaru railway.

programme.title: NEWSROOM TOKYO
programme.desc: NEWSROOM TOKYO dives deeper into the day's news with insight and analysis, from the perspective of Japan and the rest of Asia. We put a spotlight on what you need to know to understand the issues.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.sub-title: A World Where Deafness Is Not a Barrier: Kondo Ryohei / Dancer, Choreographer
programme.desc: Leading Japanese dancer and choreographer Kondo Ryohei served as director of the opening ceremony for the Tokyo Deaflympics in November 2025. He talks about the potential of physical expression.

programme.title: RAMEN JAPAN
programme.desc: Enjoy Hida Takayama Ramen! A heartwarming bowl loved in the snowy mountain town, bringing comfort with every sip.

programme.title: RAMEN JAPAN
programme.desc: All black soup! A one-of-a-kind bowl of ramen from the fertile rice lands of Toyama.

programme.title: Ukiyoe EDO-LIFE
programme.desc: It's late at night, when all are fast asleep. A young man and woman huddle together along the veranda outside of a house, attending to a ... rooster. What could the pair be up to? *"Making the Rooster Drunk to Prevent His Crowing at Dawn" (1767-68) by Suzuki Harunobu

programme.title: Hometown Stories
programme.sub-title: A Doctor Duo: Songs to Comfort the Heart
programme.desc: Two doctors in western Japan have an alter ego as the music duo Insheart, whose songs bring hope to patients and families. We follow them and the fans who find strength and comfort in their music.

programme.title: Satoyama Kitchen Diary
programme.sub-title: Veggies Patiently Pickled for the Cold Season
programme.desc: Hi! It's me, Tama. In winter, I never stray far away from my warm friend, Mr. Stove. But Grandpa Makoto and Grandma Nobuko are off to see our neighbor to learn how to make pickles. Not me. I'm staying right here. The only thing I need to know about pickles is how to eat them!

programme.title: Japanology Plus
programme.desc: Australian Phillip Richards gives back to his adopted home of Nozawaonsen by crafting gin and whiskey using local ingredients. We explore what draws him to this small village in Nagano Prefecture.

programme.sub-title: On Track for a Carbon-Free Future
programme.desc: Across the country, Japan's railways are working towards a shared goal: carbon neutrality. See the efforts being made to shift away from diesel power and to improve energy efficiency overall.

programme.title: Seasoning the Seasons
programme.desc: Hakodate, a port city located in southern Hokkaido, is known for having one of the best night views in Japan. As the surrounding seas darken, the lights of the city warm the hearts of dwellers. Beneath each light lies the lives of citizens. As the winter cold chills Hakodate, the city shines even brighter.

programme.title: Document 72 Hours
programme.sub-title: Kerosene Delivery Service in Snowy Sapporo
programme.desc: Kerosene home delivery is a key lifeline for residents of Sapporo, Hokkaido Prefecture. For three days in midwinter, we followed trucks delivering this heating oil and spoke to people who rely on this service.

programme.sub-title: Considering Our Future Through Dinosaur Research: Kobayashi Yoshitsugu / Paleontologist / Professor, Hokkaido University
programme.desc: In June 2025, Kobayashi Yoshitsugu published a trailblazing paper, illuminating the evolutionary process of large tyrannosaurs. He explains how such research helps us consider the future of humanity.

programme.title: IMPACTS: Climates Change the World
